Dara M. Tang for plaintiff and opposing party: Evely Bravos

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to deem certain matters admitted and for contempt, issue, terminating, and monetary sanctions is denied in part and granted in part. The motion to deem certain matters admitted and for contempt, issue, or terminating sanctions is denied. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to comply with the previous order and respond to defendant's attempt to informally resolve the matters in dispute. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $750 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leagve To Take Discovery After Arbitration Pursuant To Ccp 1141.24 And Ca Rule Of Court 1612 And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to conduct post-arbitration discovery is granted. Defendant may issue subpoenas for medical records pursuant to the previous agreement of the parties to limit the scope of said subpoenas. Defendant may also submit written discovery as to the issue of equitable estoppel. The defendant may propound a combination of no more than 15 interrogatories, requests for production of documents, and requests for admissions. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

TDN SWEEP
Off Calendar Mar-23-2006 Set For Trial On Jul-24-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Mtn To Quash Or Modify Subpoena
Off Calendar Mar-14-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Evelyn Bravos's motion to quash medical records subpoena is granted. As drafted, the subpoenas are overbroad. The Court does not make any rulings as to the timeliness of the subpoenas pursuant to California Rule of Court 1613. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to narrowing the scope of the requests to include a reasonable time limitation and to limit disclosure to those parts of the body or conditions that are at issue. If the parties are unable to agree, the Court will consider a motion pursuant to CCP 1141.24. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
